quick_facts.label: Provider and purpose
quick_facts.value: BARC Training Schools run OCES as a Department of Atomic Energy (DAE) recruitment-and-training route for Scientific Officer appointments following successful completion.

quick_facts.label: Duration and 2026 locations
quick_facts.value: OCES is a one-year programme. The OCES-2026 brochure states that the 2026–27 course is to be conducted at BARC Training Schools in Mumbai or Hyderabad; its tentative commencement date was July 31, 2026.

quick_facts.label: Training financial support (2026 intake)
quick_facts.value: For OCES-2026, Trainee Scientific Officers are stated to receive a stipend of ₹74,000 per month during the one-year training period plus a one-time ₹30,000 book allowance. These amounts are intake-specific and may change in later notices.

quick_facts.label: Completion outcome
quick_facts.value: For OCES-2026, a Trainee Scientific Officer must score at least 35% in each subject and 50% overall to pass; successful trainees are posted as Scientific Officer “C” in a DAE unit, with DAE retaining placement discretion including possible placement in AERB.

sections.heading: Who can apply
paragraphs: Eligibility is discipline-specific. For the 2026 cycle, engineering applicants needed an eligible B.E., B.Tech., B.Sc. (Engineering), or five-year integrated M.Tech. in one of the notified disciplines—mechanical, chemical, metallurgical, electrical, electronics, computer science, instrumentation, or civil engineering—with at least 60% aggregate marks or 6.0/10 CGPA (or equivalent). Science routes covered specified postgraduate qualifications in physics, chemistry, biosciences, geology, and geophysics, with subject prerequisites that differ by discipline.

All qualifying and pre-qualifying degrees must be regular, full-time, on-campus qualifications; correspondence, distance, research-mode, and part-time degrees are not eligible under the 2026 brochure. Applicants must be Indian citizens. For that intake, the minimum age was 18 as of August 1, 2026; maximum ages and relaxations varied by category, so applicants should use the next official brochure rather than rely on the 2026 limits.

GATE was an optional screening channel rather than the only route: eligible candidates could use the BARC online examination, a valid applicable GATE score, or both, subject to the rules for their chosen discipline.
bullets: Check the notified eligible degree title—not merely a related field—against the applicable discipline table.
For a GATE-based screening application in 2026, accepted scores were GATE 2024, 2025, or 2026 in the prescribed paper.
Government, PSU, and aided-institute employees needed a No Objection Certificate at the selection interview.

sections.heading: Length, format, and location
paragraphs: OCES is structured as one year of full-time training. BARC describes the course work as three trimesters of classroom teaching, practical training, and project work, with continuous evaluation through examinations and assignments.

The 2026–27 brochure identified Mumbai and Hyderabad as the OCES training-school locations for that cycle. Location and discipline allocations are programme-specific, so a future intake may not replicate the same options.
bullets: Three trimesters in BARC’s general course description.
For OCES-2026, the brochure listed tentative commencement on July 31, 2026.

sections.heading: What trainees study and do
paragraphs: The programme is intended to develop R&D knowledge and skills in nuclear science and technology. BARC’s general OCES description includes foundation, core, advanced, and elective courses, mini-projects, practical training, and project work.

Training is discipline-oriented. For example, the 2026 Mumbai options covered engineering design, development, operation, and maintenance of nuclear reactors, plus research in frontier basic and engineering sciences; the Hyderabad geology option focused on uranium and other atomic-mineral exploration techniques and related R&D. The precise scheme depends on the screening discipline and training-school allocation.
bullets: Foundation, core, advanced, and elective coursework.
Practical work, mini-projects, and project work.
Continuous assessment through examinations and assignments.

sections.heading: Selection and application process
paragraphs: OCES selection is a two-stage process: screening followed by a selection interview. In the 2026 cycle, screening could be through BARC’s computer-based online examination and/or an applicable GATE score; final selection was based on the selection interview, subject to medical fitness.

Applications are online through the BARC OCES/DGFS portal, and an applicant may choose only one OCES screening discipline. The 2026 schedule listed a January 21, 2026 application deadline, online tests on March 14–15, 2026, interviews from May 12 to June 12, 2026, and final OCES selections in late June. Those dates are past as of July 19, 2026 and were explicitly tentative; prospective applicants should monitor the official portal for the next intake rather than infer a recurring deadline.
bullets: Choose at least one screening option: BARC online examination, eligible GATE score, or both where permitted.
Shortlisted candidates attend selection interviews; for the 2026 cycle, most disciplines were interviewed in Mumbai, while geology and geophysics interviews were in Hyderabad.
A pre-employment medical test is required before joining.

sections.heading: Application cost, stipend, and service commitment
paragraphs: OCES is a paid training programme rather than a conventional fee-paying course. For OCES-2026, BARC specified a ₹74,000 monthly stipend and a one-time ₹30,000 book allowance. Boarding and lodging in a DAE hostel were mandatory during the OCES training period. The brochure does not present these as permanent terms, so applicants should verify financial provisions in the notice for their own intake.

For the 2026 recruitment cycle, the non-refundable application fee was ₹500 plus applicable bank charges for specified male General, OBC (non-creamy layer), Dependent-1984, and Kashmir-domicile applicants. Women, transgender applicants, SC/ST applicants, eligible dependents of defence personnel killed in action, and persons with benchmark disability as specified in the brochure were exempt. Fee rules can change by cycle.

Selected OCES-2026 candidates were required to sign an agreement and personal indemnity bond to serve DAE for at least three years after training. The bond amount consisted of stipend and the one-time book allowance actually paid; no third-party surety was required.
bullets: 2026 application fee where applicable: ₹500 plus bank charges.
Service commitment for OCES-2026: at least three years after training.

sections.heading: After OCES
paragraphs: A trainee who meets the 2026 passing standard is appointed as a Scientific Officer “C.” Allocation to a DAE unit is based on DAE programme needs and the trainee’s OCES performance; DAE may also place candidates in AERB or its regional offices. The 2026 brochure listed potential DAE destinations including BARC, IGCAR, RRCAT, VECC, HWB, NFC, BRIT, NPCIL, BHAVINI, UCIL, AMD, and DCSEM, with postings potentially at facilities elsewhere in India.

For OCES-2026, engineering trainees who completed the training and met a minimum performance criterion could enrol for an HBNI M.Tech.; other trainees could enrol for an HBNI Post-Graduate Diploma if their academic performance exceeded the specified threshold. BARC also describes OCES course work as recognized for HBNI postgraduate diploma and as course work toward certain HBNI higher degrees.

The 2026 brochure stated that appointment as Scientific Officer “C” is at Level 10, ₹56,100 in the Seventh Central Pay Commission pay matrix, with two or three performance-linked increments for OCES trainees. This is a dated 2026 pay context, not a guarantee of future compensation.
bullets: Scientific Officer “C” appointment after successful completion.
DAE determines unit and location according to organisational needs and trainee performance.
Potential HBNI M.Tech. or Post-Graduate Diploma pathway, subject to the stated performance conditions.
